mnemonics: ICE-C (Integrity Constraints Ensure Correctness): Use ICE-C to remember the main types of constraints: Integrity, Column (Domain), Entity, and Referential Constraints.
keyPoints: Domain Constraints define the permissible values for an attribute (e.g., a 'grade' attribute must be an integer between 0 and 100).
Entity Integrity requires that every relation has a primary key, and that no component of the primary key can be null.
Referential Integrity ensures that a foreign key value in one relation must either match a primary key value in another relation or be wholly null.
commonMistakes: Confusing a relational database schema (the logical design/blueprint) with a relational database instance (the actual data at a point in time).
Assuming a foreign key must have the same attribute name as the primary key it references; it only needs to have the same domain.
